L1.area: User communication and social interaction modules

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied recipient identifiers from a POST request are directly concatenated into a SQL IN clause without sanitization or type casting. An authenticated member user can inject arbitrary SQL through the recipient selection parameter, enabling blind condition-based and time-based SQL injection attacks that can exfiltrate, modify, or delete database contents.

L3.area: Electronic greeting card (ecard) sending functionality within the user communication modules
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied recipient identifiers submitted via the greeting card sending form are directly concatenated into a SQL IN clause without sanitization or type casting. An authenticated member user can inject arbitrary SQL through the recipient selection parameter when sending a photo as a greeting card, enabling blind condition-based and time-based SQL injection attacks that can exfiltrate, modify, or delete database contents.

ground_truth.reason: The ecard_recipients POST parameter values are directly concatenated into a SQL IN clause via implode() without type casting or sanitization, allowing an authenticated user to inject arbitrary SQL and compromise the database.
